output.summary: Add three new behaviors to the Accordion block: (1) 'exclusive mode' option allowing only one accordion item open at a time, (2) 'always one open' sub-option that prevents closing the last open item when exclusive mode is enabled, and (3) keep the charcoal border outline visible when an accordion item is open (currently it switches to border-accent-lighter).

fileChanges.description: Add two new parent-level attributes: exclusiveMode (boolean) and alwaysOneOpen (boolean).
fileChanges.details: Add 'exclusiveMode' with type boolean, default false. Add 'alwaysOneOpen' with type boolean, default false. These are container-level behavior controls, not inner block attributes. Update the 'example' to keep current preview working.

fileChanges.description: Add InspectorControls for exclusiveMode and alwaysOneOpen toggles. Pass data attributes to the accordion container div for JS consumption.
fileChanges.details: 1. Add a new 'Behavior' PanelBody in InspectorControls (shown for ALL variations, not just with-media). 2. Add ToggleControl for 'Only one open at a time' (exclusiveMode). 3. Add conditional ToggleControl for 'Always keep one open' (alwaysOneOpen) -- only visible when exclusiveMode is true. 4. On the accordion items wrapper div (the one with class 'flex flex-col gap-4'), add data-exclusive-mode={attributes.exclusiveMode} and data-always-one-open={attributes.alwaysOneOpen} attributes. These data attributes enable the frontend JS to read the settings.

fileChanges.description: Pass exclusiveMode and alwaysOneOpen as data attributes on the accordion items wrapper div, matching TSX output.
fileChanges.details: On the div wrapping $children (the 'flex flex-col gap-4' div), add data-exclusive-mode and data-always-one-open attributes based on the block attributes. Format: data-exclusive-mode="true" when $exclusiveMode is truthy, omit or set to "false" otherwise. Same for data-always-one-open.

fileChanges.description: Add accordion exclusive-mode and always-one-open JavaScript behavior alongside existing video playback code.
fileChanges.details: Add a new section after the existing video handler code. The JS should: 1. Find all accordion containers with data-exclusive-mode='true'. 2. For each container, listen for 'toggle' events on child <details> elements. 3. When a <details> opens and exclusive mode is on, close all sibling <details> elements. 4. When data-always-one-open='true', prevent closing the last remaining open <details> by re-opening it if the user tries to close it. Use event delegation on the container. The 'toggle' event fires on <details> elements natively when they open or close, making this straightforward without needing MutationObserver. Note: the 'toggle' event does NOT bubble in all browsers -- use capture phase or attach listeners directly to each <details> element.

fileChanges.description: Change the open-state border styling to keep charcoal outline when open.
fileChanges.details: Current classes: 'border border-charcoal rounded-3xl transition-all open:bg-accent-lighter open:border-accent-lighter not-open:hover:bg-accent not-open:hover:border-accent'. Change to: 'border border-charcoal rounded-3xl transition-all open:bg-accent-lighter not-open:hover:bg-accent not-open:hover:border-accent'. The key change is REMOVING 'open:border-accent-lighter' so the border stays charcoal when open. The background still changes to accent-lighter, providing visual feedback for the open state, but the outline remains charcoal for better definition.

fileChanges.description: Match the PHP border styling change -- remove open:border-accent-lighter from the TSX classes.
fileChanges.details: In the cn() call on the <details> element, remove 'open:border-accent-lighter' from the line that currently reads 'open:bg-accent-lighter open:border-accent-lighter'. Keep 'open:bg-accent-lighter' to maintain the background color change on open. This keeps TSX and PHP visually in sync.

attributeChanges.name: exclusiveMode
attributeChanges.description: When true, only one accordion item can be open at a time. Opening a new item automatically closes the previously open item.

attributeChanges.name: alwaysOneOpen
attributeChanges.description: When true (and exclusiveMode is also true), prevents closing the last remaining open accordion item. At least one item is always expanded. Only effective when exclusiveMode is enabled.

designDecisions.question: Where should the exclusive-mode and always-one-open toggles appear in the editor?
designDecisions.recommendation: Add a new 'Behavior' PanelBody in InspectorControls, visible for ALL accordion variations (not gated behind isWithMedia). This is the standard location for block behavior settings.
designDecisions.evidence: Other blocks in this theme use InspectorControls > PanelBody for behavior toggles.

designDecisions.question: How should the 'toggle' event be handled given that it does not bubble in all browsers?
designDecisions.recommendation: Attach individual 'toggle' event listeners to each <details> element within the accordion container, rather than relying on event delegation. This ensures cross-browser compatibility. Use a flag variable to prevent recursive toggle handling when programmatically closing sibling items.
designDecisions.evidence: The 'toggle' event on <details> elements does not bubble in Safari. Direct attachment is the reliable approach.

designDecisions.question: Should the open accordion item keep the charcoal border, or should a different border color be used?
designDecisions.recommendation: Remove open:border-accent-lighter so the border stays as border-charcoal when open. The accent-lighter background (open:bg-accent-lighter) still provides clear visual distinction for the open state. The charcoal outline gives better visual definition and separation between items.
designDecisions.evidence: User explicitly requested 'Keep accordion outline when open'. Current behavior switches border to accent-lighter which is very close to the background color, making the outline effectively invisible.

designDecisions.question: When exclusive mode is toggled on and multiple items are already open, what should happen?
designDecisions.recommendation: The JS should only enforce exclusive behavior on new toggle actions. If multiple items are already open when the page loads (via openByDefault on multiple items), the JS should not auto-close them on page load. The exclusive behavior activates on the next user interaction. This prevents jarring content shifts on initial render.
designDecisions.evidence: Progressive enhancement principle -- JS enhances but does not break initial server-rendered state.

designDecisions.question: Should the alwaysOneOpen option auto-open the first item if none are open by default?
designDecisions.recommendation: No. The alwaysOneOpen behavior should only prevent the user from closing the last remaining open item. If no items have openByDefault=true, none will be open on initial render. The always-one-open constraint activates after the user first opens an item. This keeps the server-rendered HTML predictable and controlled by the openByDefault attribute.
designDecisions.evidence: The openByDefault attribute on AccordionItem already handles initial open state. Mixing two sources of truth for initial state would create confusion.

jsImplementationNotes.eventHandling: Use the 'toggle' event on <details> elements. Attach listeners directly to each <details> (not via delegation) for Safari compatibility.
jsImplementationNotes.exclusiveModeLogic: When a <details> opens (event.target.open === true), iterate sibling <details> elements and set open = false on each. Use a processing flag to prevent recursive handling.
jsImplementationNotes.alwaysOneOpenLogic: When a <details> closes (event.target.open === false), check if any sibling <details> is still open. If none are open and alwaysOneOpen is true, re-open the one that just closed (event.target.open = true). Must also use the processing flag here.
jsImplementationNotes.dataAttributeContract: The JS reads data-exclusive-mode and data-always-one-open from the accordion items wrapper div. Values are 'true' or absent/false.
jsImplementationNotes.noEditorJs: These behaviors are frontend-only. In the editor, the native <details> behavior is fine since the editor is for content editing, not behavior preview.

qaChecklist: Exclusive mode: open item A, then open item B -- item A should close
Exclusive mode: open item A, close item A -- no items should be open (unless alwaysOneOpen)
Always-one-open + exclusive: open item A, try to close item A -- item A should remain open
Always-one-open + exclusive: open item A, open item B -- item A closes, item B stays open
Without exclusive mode: multiple items can be open simultaneously (default behavior preserved)
Open state styling: when an item is open, the border should remain charcoal (not accent-lighter)
Open state styling: when an item is open, the background should still be accent-lighter
Hover state: non-open items should still show bg-accent and border-accent on hover
Editor: both toggle controls appear in InspectorControls for default and with-media variations
Editor: alwaysOneOpen toggle is only visible when exclusiveMode is enabled
Multiple openByDefault items: if exclusive mode is on but 2 items have openByDefault, both render open on page load (JS does not auto-close on init)
